column1: # PROTECTED.tsv -- machine-checkable protection manifest for the capacity programme.
# Owner: docs/plans/capacity_programme/flexnn-package.md (FP-0 seeds it; only that plan's tasks amend it).
# WHAT THIS IS. A path listed here is working research code or a research architecture that is NOT a
# duplicate and NOT dead, regardless of what a zero-caller sweep says. Deleting or rewriting it
# breaks the paper trail of a result that is already on disk.
#   1. Any task whose write set touches code MUST check its diff against this file before landing:
#      Any output = STOP, escalate to the user. Empty output = clear.
#   2. A protected path may be given a re-export shim (the automl_package/examples/convergence.py
#      precedent) so its symbols keep resolving. A shim is not a deletion.
#   3. Removing a row requires an explicit user ruling recorded in the amending task.
#   symbol-or-*  '*' means the whole file is protected; a name means that symbol specifically.
#   certified-artifact-dir  where the results this code produced live; '-' if it produces no
#                           artifacts of its own (it is a library for a driver that does).
# Every path and every symbol below was confirmed on disk 2026-07-20 (path existence + grep for the
# symbol). Line numbers are deliberately NOT recorded -- they rot; grep the symbol name instead.

column3: The original scalar-input router. FIVE scripts import this module directly by bare name (re-derive with the FP-7 grep; never trust a count). One of them, sinc_width_experiment.py, produced the certified width result. Preregistration documents under capacity_ladder_results/{S1,T2,H1,W1,W2}/PREREGISTRATION.md cite these exact names.
Hard-label CE *or* soft-target CE training. The package router has no soft-target path -- it is a strict behavioural subset. Replacing this implementation drops working machinery.
Soft-target construction consumed by s1/h1/sinc. No package equivalent.
Trains the router directly on the deployment metric rather than on a derived label. UNRESOLVED research arm: merging it ships an unadjudicated method as a default; deleting it discards the arm.

The five-arm label-construction factorial (soft, soft_no_prior, soft_smoothed, hard_knee, raw_argmax) -- a certified comparison. The library implements only its winner; the other four arms exist nowhere else.
Independent vector-input router port. Kept until FP-5 has demonstrated the package router reproduces its numbers.
Vector-input soft-target trainer. No package equivalent.
Blend-likelihood evaluation. The package router only hard-routes and scores the routed choice -- it has NO blend path at all.
Blend-likelihood evaluation metric. No package equivalent.
Paired routed/blend evaluation harness the T2 preregistration cites by name.
Group word-problem composer -- a different problem domain, forced by a theorem. Protocol, not library; does not belong in automl_package/models/.
Group word-problem composer (graded variant). Same reason.
Superseded width architecture (ResidualCascadeNet). Retained, NOT migrated, NOT deleted: results sit on disk and deleting the code orphans them from their producer.
Driver for both superseded arms; writes RESULTS_DIR_CASCADE and RESULTS_DIR_MATRYOSHKA. Deleting it orphans both artifact directories.
Produced the certified width selection numbers via its selector machinery, run inside kdropout_converged_width_experiment.py. Also the source of the label rule the package router copied.

Holds the certified width architectures, including SharedTrunkPerWidthHeadNet -- the architecture of record. FP-2 MOVES these into the package and leaves a re-export shim; the file itself is never deleted while any driver imports it by bare name.
